issues.title: PromptChatMemoryAdvisor rerenders prompt, causing template errors
issues.body:
**Bug description**
`PromptChatMemoryAdvisor` seems to call `PromptTemplate.render()` even though the prompt has already been rendered.

**Environment**
This applies to Spring AI M8 (tested on macOS with Amazon Corretto 21). I haven't encountered this issue with Spring AI M7.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Define a `ChatClient` like this:

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(
			new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(MessageWindowChatMemory
				.builder()
				.maxMessages(10)
				.build()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
		.build();
}
```

2. Define `user.st` like this:

```
How would you answer the following question:
{question}
```

3. Now call the following:

```java
String response = chatClient
	.prompt()
	.user(user -> user.param("question", "{,}"))
	.call()
	.content();
```

You now get the following exception:

```
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: The template string is not valid.
	at org.springframework.ai.template.st.StTemplateRenderer.createST(StTemplateRenderer.java:100) ~[spring-ai-template-st-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.template.st.StTemplateRenderer.apply(StTemplateRenderer.java:85) ~[spring-ai-template-st-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.prompt.PromptTemplate.render(PromptTemplate.java:155) ~[spring-ai-model-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.api.AdvisedRequest.toPrompt(AdvisedRequest.java:247) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.api.AdvisedRequest.toChatClientRequest(AdvisedRequest.java:209) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.nextAroundCall(DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.java:147) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.PromptChatMemoryAdvisor.aroundCall(PromptChatMemoryAdvisor.java:90) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.lambda$nextCall$1(DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.java:124) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at io.micrometer.observation.Observation.observe(Observation.java:564) ~[micrometer-observation-1.14.6.jar:1.14.6]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.advisor.DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.nextCall(DefaultAroundAdvisorChain.java:119) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.lambda$doGetObservableChatClientResponse$1(DefaultChatClient.java:513) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at io.micrometer.observation.Observation.observe(Observation.java:564) ~[micrometer-observation-1.14.6.jar:1.14.6]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.doGetObservableChatClientResponse(DefaultChatClient.java:511) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.doGetObservableChatClientResponse(DefaultChatClient.java:494) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at org.springframework.ai.chat.client.DefaultChatClient$DefaultCallResponseSpec.content(DefaultChatClient.java:489) ~[spring-ai-client-chat-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	at com.example.demo.DemoApplication.lambda$runner$1(DemoApplication.java:45) ~[classes/:na]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.lambda$callRunner$4(SpringApplication.java:785) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.util.function.ThrowingConsumer$1.acceptWithException(ThrowingConsumer.java:82) ~[spring-core-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.util.function.ThrowingConsumer.accept(ThrowingConsumer.java:60) ~[spring-core-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.util.function.ThrowingConsumer$1.accept(ThrowingConsumer.java:86) ~[spring-core-6.2.6.jar:6.2.6]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.callRunner(SpringApplication.java:797) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.callRunner(SpringApplication.java:785) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.lambda$callRunners$3(SpringApplication.java:773) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp$OfRef.accept(ForEachOps.java:184) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.SortedOps$SizedRefSortingSink.end(SortedOps.java:357) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:510) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:499) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp.evaluateSequential(ForEachOps.java:151) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp$OfRef.evaluateSequential(ForEachOps.java:174) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:234) ~[na:na]
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.forEach(ReferencePipeline.java:596) ~[na:na]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.callRunners(SpringApplication.java:773) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:325) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:1362) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:1351) ~[spring-boot-3.4.5.jar:3.4.5]
	at com.example.demo.DemoApplication.main(DemoApplication.java:23) ~[classes/:na]
Caused by: org.stringtemplate.v4.compiler.STException: null
	at org.stringtemplate.v4.compiler.Compiler.reportMessageAndThrowSTException(Compiler.java:224) ~[ST4-4.3.4.jar:na]
	at org.stringtemplate.v4.compiler.Compiler.compile(Compiler.java:154) ~[ST4-4.3.4.jar:na]
	at org.stringtemplate.v4.STGroup.compile(STGroup.java:514) ~[ST4-4.3.4.jar:na]
	at org.stringtemplate.v4.ST.<init>(ST.java:162) ~[ST4-4.3.4.jar:na]
	at org.stringtemplate.v4.ST.<init>(ST.java:156) ~[ST4-4.3.4.jar:na]
	at org.springframework.ai.template.st.StTemplateRenderer.createST(StTemplateRenderer.java:97) ~[spring-ai-template-st-1.0.0-M8.jar:1.0.0-M8]
	... 35 common frames omitted
```

If you put a breakpoint in the code, you can see that `StTemplateRenferer.createST()` is called with a `template` containing: 

```
How would you answer the following question:
{,}
```

My guess is that due to `template` containing an already rendered template, it's interpreting `{,}` as another template literal, which results in invalid syntax.  I also assume this is potentially unsafe, because if the `question` parameter comes from user input, wouldn't they be able to do "stringtemplate injection"?

**Expected behavior**
I don't expect an `IllegalArgumentException` to be thrown. If I roll back to Spring AI M7 and replace the `PromptChatMemoryAdvisor` with the `InMemoryChatMemory`, it works as expected:

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(new InMemoryChatMemory()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
		.build();
}
```

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
See steps to reproduce

comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this issue! In M8, the userText is rendered by `ChatClient` when building the `Prompt` object before calling the advisor chain so to ensure consistency and predictability throughout the execution flow.

The default template engine uses `{}` as delimiters for placeholders to be replaced. If the user text includes any of those characters, they will be considered candidate for templating. If you want to allow a user to use those characters in their question, you can customise the default template engine configuration to use different characters as placeholder delimiters via the new `.defaultTemplateRenderer()` method.

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(
			new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(MessageWindowChatMemory
				.builder()
				.maxMessages(10)
				.build()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
                .defaultTemplateRenderer(StTemplateRenderer.builder().startDelimiterToken('<').endDelimiterToken('>').build())
		.build();
}
```

where `user.st` is changed as follows:

```java
How would you answer the following question:
<question>
```

In alternative, you might be able to keep using the default delimiters, but disable the template validation logic (`ValidationMode.NONE`) or let it log a warning instead of throwing an exception (`ValidationMode.WARN`). Though, I would recommend the previous approach.

```java
@Bean
ChatClient chatClient(ChatClient.Builder builder) {
	return builder
		.defaultAdvisors(
			new PromptChatMemoryAdvisor(MessageWindowChatMemory
				.builder()
				.maxMessages(10)
				.build()))
		.defaultUser(new ClassPathResource("user.st"))
                .defaultTemplateRenderer(StTemplateRenderer.builder().validationMode(ValidationMode.NONE).build())
		.build();
}
```

I hope that helps!

Changing the validation mode didn't work for me, but changing the delimiter did.

The reason I noticed it is because one of my parameters contained JSON. So I guess this is also important to take into account when using a structured output, considering that it results in a JSON schema being added to the prompt.

@g00glen00b that's right, when using the output converters via ChatClient, the JSON schema might cause some templating issue if the prompt is rendered again. In the current SNAPSHOT (future RC1), that issue has been solved for the structured output. But it's still something to consider if curly braces are desired in the system or user prompt.

issues.title: Uploading PDF documents as part of the Prompt to OpenAI
issues.body:
**Expected Behavior**
Perhaps I am not doing this correctly. If so, please point me in the direction.

OpenAI GPT-4 and on allow you to upload a PDF as part of the chat request. So, something like this should be possible:

```
byte[] fileData = ...
Media media = Media.builder().data(fileData).mimeType(DOC_PDF).build();
Prompt prompt = new Prompt(
        List.of(new UserMessage("extract text from the following PDF document."),
            new UserMessage("some text here", media)), openAiChatOptions);

CallResponseSpec callResponseSpec = chatClient.prompt(prompt).call();
ChatResponse chatResponse = callResponseSpec.chatResponse();
```
**Current Behavior**

While the above compiles, even with GPT-4o (which supports this), I get the following error:

```
org.springframework.ai.retry.NonTransientAiException: 400 - {
  "error": {
    "message": "Invalid MIME type. Only image types are supported.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": null,
    "code": "invalid_image_format"
  }
}
```

<!--- 
How has this issue affected you?
What are you trying to accomplish?
What other alternatives have you considered?
Are you aware of any workarounds?
-->

Pretty clear - can't use a PDF document as part of a UserMessage. I am not currently aware of a workaround, but if there is one, please point me to it.

Much appreciated!

comments.body: > OpenAI GPT-4 and on allow you to upload a PDF as part of the chat request Where did you find this information? Based on the [API specification](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create), it seems that only text, image, and audio inputs are allowed.

I've been looking for information on this and it looks like it's possible using the assistants api.

- related doc
  - https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/assistants
  - https://stackoverflow.com/a/78924474/29672082

Currently, the assistants api is in beta.
As far as I know, spring ai does not support the assistants api yet.

Here is the page that shows you a couple of ways of doing, including the one I was interested in, which is embedding base64 encoded...
https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/pdf-files?api-mode=responses

Under Supported Models it says this:
Only models that support both text and image inputs, such as gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, or o1, can accept PDF files as input.

Thank you for the information.
I think we'd probably need to add a feature to make it work that way

BTW, the assistants api is going to be deprecated.  As per the openai [page](https://platform.openai.com/docs/assistants/overview) 

```
Based on your feedback from the Assistants API beta, we've incorporated key improvements 
into the Responses API. After we achieve full feature parity, we will announce a deprecation
 plan later this year, with a target sunset date in the first half of 2026. 
[Learn more](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/responses-vs-chat-completions).
```

There are some docs on this - https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/multimodality.html#_spring_ai_multimodality

also test case here - https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/bfcaad7b5495c5927a62b44169e8713e044c2497/models/spring-ai-openai/src/test/java/org/springframework/ai/openai/chat/client/OpenAiChatClientIT.java#L308


if that doesn't solve your problem, please reopen the issue.

@markpollack IN the IT that you referenced, this line:
`.media(MimeTypeUtils.IMAGE_PNG, new ClassPathResource("/test.png")`

The `MimeTypeUtils` class doesn't have PDF as an option. Trying to substitute this:
```
.media(MimeType.valueOf("application/pdf"),
                new ClassPathResource("/file.pdf")
```

(`MypeType` is `org.springframework.util.MimeType`)

When I do that, I get the same error response back:
```
org.springframework.ai.retry.NonTransientAiException: 400 - {
  "error": {
    "message": "Invalid MIME type. Only image types are supported.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "param": null,
    "code": "invalid_image_format"
  }
}
```

Also, I do not have permissions to reopen the issue.

issues.title: [OpenSearch] Bug: Cannot connect to multiple OpenSearch hosts with credentials
